1→/**
     2→ * UI Walking Skeleton: Agent Delegation Across Three Surfaces
     3→ *
     4→ * Verifies the minimal E2E path through all three UI surfaces:
     5→ *   Surface 1 (Task Popup): assign button -> status badge
     6→ *   Surface 2 (Governance Feed): review-ready item appears
     7→ *   Surface 3 (Agent Review View): diff + accept
     8→ *
     9→ * Driving ports: POST /assign, GET /sessions/:id, GET /feed,
    10→ *   GET /review, POST /accept, POST /reject
    11→ *
    12→ * Stories: US-0.4, US-0.5, US-1.2, US-2.1, US-2.2, US-2.3
    13→ */
    14→import { describe, it, expect } from "bun:test";
    15→import {
    16→  setupOrchestratorSuite,
    17→  createTestUser,
    18→  createTestWorkspace,
    19→  createReadyTask,
    20→  assignTaskToAgent,
    21→  getSessionStatus,
    22→  openTaskPopup,
    23→  isAssignButtonVisible,
    24→  getAgentBadgeText,
    25→  getGovernanceFeed,
    26→  findFeedItemForTask,
    27→  feedItemHasActions,
    28→  openReviewView,
    29→  reviewShowsTaskContent,
    30→  acceptAgentWork,
    31→  rejectWithFeedback,
    32→  getTaskStatus,
    33→  simulateAgentCompletion,
    34→  simulateAgentError,
    35→  simulateAgentActive,
    36→  waitForCondition,
    37→  type TestUser,
    38→  type TestWorkspace,
    39→  type TestTask,
    40→} from "./orchestrator-ui-test-kit";
    41→
    42→const getRuntime = setupOrchestratorSuite("ui-walking-skeleton");
    43→
    44→describe("UI Walking Skeleton: Agent Delegation Across Three Surfaces", () => {
    45→  let user: TestUser;
    46→  let workspace: TestWorkspace;
    47→
    48→  // US-0.4, US-0.5, US-1.2, US-2.1, US-2.2
    49→  it("assigns from popup, monitors in feed, accepts in review view", async () => {
    50→    const runtime = getRuntime();
    51→    user = await createTestUser(runtime.baseUrl, "ws1");
    52→    workspace = await createTestWorkspace(runtime.baseUrl, user);
    53→
    54→    // Given a task with status "ready"
    55→    const taskTitle = `Implement input validation ${Date.now()}`;
    56→    const task = await createReadyTask(
    57→      runtime.surreal,
    58→      workspace.workspaceId,
    59→      { title: taskTitle, status: "ready" },
    60→    );
    61→
    62→    // Surface 1: Task Popup -- "Assign to Agent" button visible
    63→    const detailBefore = await openTaskPopup(
    64→      runtime.baseUrl,
    65→      user,
    66→      workspace.workspaceId,
    67→      task.taskId,
    68→    );
    69→    expect(isAssignButtonVisible(detailBefore)).toBe(true);
    70→
    71→    // When I assign the task to an agent
    72→    const assignment = await assignTaskToAgent(
    73→      runtime.baseUrl,
    74→      user,
    75→      workspace.workspaceId,
    76→      task.taskId,
    77→    );
    78→    expect(assignment.agentSessionId).toBeDefined();
    79→
    80→    // Simulate agent transitioning from spawning to active
    81→    await simulateAgentActive(runtime, assignment.agentSessionId);
    82→
    83→    // Then the popup shows a status badge "Agent working"
    84→    const detailAfterAssign = await openTaskPopup(
    85→      runtime.baseUrl,
    86→      user,
    87→      workspace.workspaceId,
    88→      task.taskId,
    89→    );
    90→    expect(getAgentBadgeText(detailAfterAssign)).toBe("Agent working");
    91→
    92→    // Simulate agent completing work (transitions to idle)
    93→    await simulateAgentCompletion(runtime, assignment.agentSessionId);
    94→
    95→    // Surface 2: Governance Feed -- review-ready item appears
    96→    const feed = await getGovernanceFeed(
    97→      runtime.baseUrl,
    98→      user,
    99→      workspace.workspaceId,
   100→    );
   101→    const feedResult = findFeedItemForTask(feed, taskTitle);
   102→    expect(feedResult).toBeDefined();
   103→    expect(feedResult!.tier).toBe("review");
   104→    expect(
   105→      feedItemHasActions(feedResult!.item, ["Review", "Abort"]),
   106→    ).toBe(true);
   107→
   108→    // Surface 3: Agent Review View -- task title and accept
   109→    const review = await openReviewView(
   110→      runtime.baseUrl,
   111→      user,
   112→      workspace.workspaceId,
   113→      assignment.agentSessionId,
   114→    );
   115→    expect(reviewShowsTaskContent(review, taskTitle)).toBe(
   116→      true,
   117→    );
   118→
   119→    // When I accept the agent's work
   120→    const acceptResult = await acceptAgentWork(
   121→      runtime.baseUrl,
   122→      user,
   123→      workspace.workspaceId,
   124→      assignment.agentSessionId,
   125→    );
   126→    expect(acceptResult.accepted).toBe(true);
   127→
   128→    // Task status remains "ready" — accept completes the session, not the task
   129→    const finalStatus = await getTaskStatus(runtime.surreal, task.taskId);
   130→    expect(finalStatus).toBe("ready");
   131→  }, 60_000);
   132→
   133→  // US-2.3
   134→  it("rejects agent work with feedback and sees agent resume", async () => {
   135→    const runtime = getRuntime();
   136→    user = await createTestUser(runtime.baseUrl, "ws2");
   137→    workspace = await createTestWorkspace(runtime.baseUrl, user);
   138→
   139→    // Given a task assigned to an agent with completed work
   140→    const task = await createReadyTask(
   141→      runtime.surreal,
   142→      workspace.workspaceId,
   143→      { title: `Fix login bug ${Date.now()}`, status: "ready" },
   144→    );
   145→    const assignment = await assignTaskToAgent(
   146→      runtime.baseUrl,
   147→      user,
   148→      workspace.workspaceId,
   149→      task.taskId,
   150→    );
   151→    await simulateAgentCompletion(runtime, assignment.agentSessionId);
   152→
   153→    // When I reject the work with feedback
   154→    const rejectResult = await rejectWithFeedback(
   155→      runtime.baseUrl,
   156→      user,
   157→      workspace.workspaceId,
   158→      assignment.agentSessionId,
   159→      "Please add input validation for email field",
   160→    );
   161→    expect(rejectResult.rejected).toBe(true);
   162→    expect(rejectResult.continuing).toBe(true);
   163→
   164→    // Then the task popup badge shows "Agent working"
   165→    const detailAfterReject = await openTaskPopup(
   166→      runtime.baseUrl,
   167→      user,
   168→      workspace.workspaceId,
   169→      task.taskId,
   170→    );
   171→    expect(getAgentBadgeText(detailAfterReject)).toBe("Agent working");
   172→  }, 60_000);
   173→
   174→  // US-1.2
   175→  it("surfaces agent error as blocking feed item", async () => {
   176→    const runtime = getRuntime();
   177→    user = await createTestUser(runtime.baseUrl, "ws3");
   178→    workspace = await createTestWorkspace(runtime.baseUrl, user);
   179→
   180→    // Given a task assigned to an agent
   181→    const taskTitle = `Refactor auth module ${Date.now()}`;
   182→    const task = await createReadyTask(
   183→      runtime.surreal,
   184→      workspace.workspaceId,
   185→      { title: taskTitle, status: "ready" },
   186→    );
   187→    const assignment = await assignTaskToAgent(
   188→      runtime.baseUrl,
   189→      user,
   190→      workspace.workspaceId,
   191→      task.taskId,
   192→    );
   193→
   194→    // When the agent encounters an error
   195→    await simulateAgentError(
   196→      runtime,
   197→      assignment.agentSessionId,
   198→      "Out of memory during compilation",
   199→    );
   200→
   201→    // Then the governance feed shows a blocking item
   202→    const feed = await getGovernanceFeed(
   203→      runtime.baseUrl,
   204→      user,
   205→      workspace.workspaceId,
   206→    );
   207→    const feedResult = findFeedItemForTask(feed, taskTitle);
   208→    expect(feedResult).toBeDefined();
   209→    expect(feedResult!.tier).toBe("blocking");
   210→    expect(feedResult!.item.reason).toContain(
   211→      "Out of memory during compilation",
   212→    );
   213→  }, 60_000);
   214→});
   215→

1→/**
     2→ * Orchestrator UI Acceptance Test Kit
     3→ *
     4→ * Extends the orchestrator-test-kit with UI-surface-specific helpers.
     5→ * Tests invoke through the same HTTP driving ports as backend tests,
     6→ * but assert UI-observable outcomes (feed items, review data, session status).
     7→ *
     8→ * Driving ports used:
     9→ *   - POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/assign (assign task to agent)
    10→ *   - GET  /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id (session status)
    11→ *   - GET  /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/review (review data)
    12→ *   - POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/accept (accept work)
    13→ *   - POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/reject (reject with feedback)
    14→ *   - POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/abort (abort session)
    15→ *   - GET  /api/workspaces/:ws/feed (governance feed)
    16→ *   - GET  /api/entities/:entityId (entity detail for task popup)
    17→ */
    18→
    19→import {
    20→  setupOrchestratorSuite,
    21→  createTestUser,
    22→  createTestWorkspace,
    23→  createReadyTask,
    24→  createTestProject,
    25→  assignTaskToAgent,
    26→  getSessionStatus,
    27→  acceptAgentWork,
    28→  rejectWithFeedback,
    29→  abortAgentSession,
    30→  getReviewSummary,
    31→  getTaskStatus,
    32→  fetchJson,
    33→  fetchRaw,
    34→  type OrchestratorTestRuntime,
    35→  type TestUser,
    36→  type TestWorkspace,
    37→  type TestTask,
    38→  type TestProject,
    39→  type AssignmentResponse,
    40→  type SessionStatusResponse,
    41→  type ReviewResponse,
    42→} from "../coding-agent-orchestrator/orchestrator-test-kit";
    43→import type {
    44→  GovernanceFeedResponse,
    45→  GovernanceFeedItem,
    46→  EntityDetailResponse,
    47→} from "../../../app/src/shared/contracts";
    48→
    49→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50→// Re-exports for convenience
    51→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    52→
    53→export {
    54→  setupOrchestratorSuite,
    55→  createTestUser,
    56→  createTestWorkspace,
    57→  createReadyTask,
    58→  createTestProject,
    59→  assignTaskToAgent,
    60→  getSessionStatus,
    61→  acceptAgentWork,
    62→  rejectWithFeedback,
    63→  abortAgentSession,
    64→  getReviewSummary,
    65→  getTaskStatus,
    66→  fetchJson,
    67→  fetchRaw,
    68→};
    69→
    70→export type {
    71→  OrchestratorTestRuntime,
    72→  TestUser,
    73→  TestWorkspace,
    74→  TestTask,
    75→  TestProject,
    76→  AssignmentResponse,
    77→  SessionStatusResponse,
    78→  ReviewResponse,
    79→};
    80→
    81→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    82→// Surface 1: Task Popup Helpers
    83→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    84→
    85→/**
    86→ * Fetches entity detail for a task (simulates opening the task popup).
    87→ * This is the driving port for Surface 1.
    88→ */
    89→export async function openTaskPopup(
    90→  baseUrl: string,
    91→  user: TestUser,
    92→  workspaceId: string,
    93→  taskId: string,
    94→): Promise<EntityDetailResponse> {
    95→  const entityId = `task:${taskId}`;
    96→  return fetchJson<EntityDetailResponse>(
    97→    `${baseUrl}/api/entities/${encodeURIComponent(entityId)}?workspaceId=${encodeURIComponent(workspaceId)}`,
    98→    { headers: user.headers },
    99→  );
   100→}
   101→
   102→/**
   103→ * Checks whether the task popup would show an assign button.
   104→ * Business rule: assign button visible when task status is "ready" or "todo"
   105→ * and no active agent session exists.
   106→ */
   107→export function isAssignButtonVisible(
   108→  detail: EntityDetailResponse,
   109→): boolean {
   110→  const status = detail.entity.data.status as string | undefined;
   111→  const hasActiveSession = detail.agentSession !== undefined;
   112→  return (
   113→    detail.entity.kind === "task" &&
   114→    (status === "ready" || status === "todo") &&
   115→    !hasActiveSession
   116→  );
   117→}
   118→
   119→/**
   120→ * Checks whether the task popup would show a review button.
   121→ * Business rule: review button visible when agent session is idle.
   122→ */
   123→export function isReviewButtonVisible(
   124→  detail: EntityDetailResponse,
   125→): boolean {
   126→  return detail.agentSession?.orchestratorStatus === "idle";
   127→}
   128→
   129→/**
   130→ * Extracts the agent status badge text from entity detail.
   131→ */
   132→export function getAgentBadgeText(
   133→  detail: EntityDetailResponse,
   134→): string | undefined {
   135→  const session = detail.agentSession;
   136→  if (!session) return undefined;
   137→
   138→  const statusMap: Record<string, string> = {
   139→    spawning: "Starting agent...",
   140→    active: "Agent working",
   141→    idle: "Review ready",
   142→    completed: "Agent done",
   143→    error: "Agent error",
   144→    aborted: "Aborted",
   145→  };
   146→  return statusMap[session.orchestratorStatus];
   147→}
   148→
   149→/**
   150→ * Extracts file change count from entity detail agent session data.
   151→ */
   152→export function getFileChangeCount(
   153→  detail: EntityDetailResponse,
   154→): number {
   155→  return detail.agentSession?.filesChangedCount ?? 0;
   156→}
   157→
   158→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   159→// Surface 2: Governance Feed Helpers
   160→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   161→
   162→/**
   163→ * Fetches the governance feed (simulates viewing the feed panel).
   164→ * This is the driving port for Surface 2.
   165→ */
   166→export async function getGovernanceFeed(
   167→  baseUrl: string,
   168→  user: TestUser,
   169→  workspaceId: string,
   170→): Promise<GovernanceFeedResponse> {
   171→  return fetchJson<GovernanceFeedResponse>(
   172→    `${baseUrl}/api/workspaces/${encodeURIComponent(workspaceId)}/feed`,
   173→    { headers: user.headers },
   174→  );
   175→}
   176→
   177→/**
   178→ * Finds a feed item matching a task name across all tiers.
   179→ */
   180→export function findFeedItemForTask(
   181→  feed: GovernanceFeedResponse,
   182→  taskName: string,
   183→): { item: GovernanceFeedItem; tier: string } | undefined {
   184→  for (const [tier, items] of Object.entries(feed)) {
   185→    if (tier === "updatedAt") continue;
   186→    for (const item of items as GovernanceFeedItem[]) {
   187→      if (item.entityName === taskName || item.reason.includes(taskName)) {
   188→        return { item, tier };
   189→      }
   190→    }
   191→  }
   192→  return undefined;
   193→}
   194→
   195→/**
   196→ * Checks that a feed item has the expected actions.
   197→ */
   198→export function feedItemHasActions(
   199→  item: GovernanceFeedItem,
   200→  expectedActions: string[],
   201→): boolean {
   202→  const actionLabels = item.actions.map((a) => a.label);
   203→  return expectedActions.every((ea) => actionLabels.includes(ea));
   204→}
   205→
   206→/**
   207→ * Extracts the agent session ID from a feed item (for navigation to review view).
   208→ */
   209→export function getSessionIdFromFeedItem(
   210→  item: GovernanceFeedItem,
   211→): string | undefined {
   212→  // The agentSessionId will be added as metadata on agent-related feed items
   213→  return (item as GovernanceFeedItem & { agentSessionId?: string })
   214→    .agentSessionId;
   215→}
   216→
   217→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   218→// Surface 3: Agent Review View Helpers
   219→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   220→
   221→/**
   222→ * Opens the review view by fetching review data (simulates navigating
   223→ * to /workspace/:ws/review/:sessionId).
   224→ * This is the driving port for Surface 3.
   225→ */
   226→export async function openReviewView(
   227→  baseUrl: string,
   228→  user: TestUser,
   229→  workspaceId: string,
   230→  sessionId: string,
   231→): Promise<ReviewResponse> {
   232→  return getReviewSummary(baseUrl, user, workspaceId, sessionId);
   233→}
   234→
   235→/**
   236→ * Asserts review data contains expected content for a task.
   237→ */
   238→export function reviewShowsTaskContent(
   239→  review: ReviewResponse,
   240→  expectedTitle: string,
   241→): boolean {
   242→  return review.taskTitle === expectedTitle;
   243→}
   244→
   245→/**

to:
```sql
SELECT role FROM member_of WHERE in IN $person<-identity_person<-identity AND out = $ws LIMIT 1;
```

The old query checked if the person was directly the `in` side of `member_of`. But the identity bootstrap creates the edge as `identity → member_of → workspace`, not `person → member_of → workspace`. So the old query was already wrong — but the new query using graph traversal `$person<-identity_person<-identity` should fix it... unless the traversal direction is backwards.

The bootstrap creates: `RELATE identity → identity_person → person`

Now the query: `$person<-identity_person<-identity`

In SurrealDB graph traversal syntax `A<-edge<-node`:
- `$person<-identity_person` means: from $person, traverse INCOMING identity_person edges. Since identity_person has IN=identity, OUT=person, and $person is the OUT side, incoming edges to $person would give us the identity records (IN side).

But the bootstrap creates `identity` as the IN side instead, so that query was already broken. The new approach tries to fix this by traversing from person through the identity_person relation to find the actual identities, then checking their member_of edges.

Now I'm wondering whether SurrealDB actually supports inline graph traversal like `$person<-identity_person<-identity` inside a WHERE clause with the IN operator. I could use a LET statement to extract just the identity IDs first, then use those in the WHERE clause with the IN operator, though that would require splitting this into multiple statements which the current code should support.

1→/**
     2→ * UI Walking Skeleton: Agent Delegation Across Three Surfaces
     3→ *
     4→ * Verifies the minimal E2E path through all three UI surfaces:
     5→ *   Surface 1 (Task Popup): assign button -> status badge
     6→ *   Surface 2 (Governance Feed): review-ready item appears
     7→ *   Surface 3 (Agent Review View): diff + accept
     8→ *
     9→ * Driving ports: POST /assign, GET /sessions/:id, GET /feed,
    10→ *   GET /review, POST /accept, POST /reject
    11→ *
    12→ * Stories: US-0.4, US-0.5, US-1.2, US-2.1, US-2.2, US-2.3
    13→ */
    14→import { describe, it, expect } from "bun:test";
    15→import {
    16→  setupOrchestratorSuite,
    17→  createTestUser,
    18→  createTestWorkspace,
    19→  createReadyTask,
    20→  assignTaskToAgent,
    21→  getSessionStatus,
    22→  openTaskPopup,
    23→  isAssignButtonVisible,
    24→  getAgentBadgeText,
    25→  getGovernanceFeed,
    26→  findFeedItemForTask,
    27→  feedItemHasActions,
    28→  openReviewView,
    29→  reviewShowsTaskContent,
    30→  acceptAgentWork,
    31→  rejectWithFeedback,
    32→  getTaskStatus,
    33→  simulateAgentCompletion,
    34→  simulateAgentError,
    35→  simulateAgentActive,
    36→  waitForCondition,
    37→  type TestUser,
    38→  type TestWorkspace,
    39→  type TestTask,
    40→} from "./orchestrator-ui-test-kit";
    41→
    42→const getRuntime = setupOrchestratorSuite("ui-walking-skeleton");
    43→
    44→describe("UI Walking Skeleton: Agent Delegation Across Three Surfaces", () => {
    45→  let user: TestUser;
    46→  let workspace: TestWorkspace;
    47→
    48→  // US-0.4, US-0.5, US-1.2, US-2.1, US-2.2
    49→  it("assigns from popup, monitors in feed, accepts in review view", async () => {
    50→    const runtime = getRuntime();
    51→    user = await createTestUser(runtime.baseUrl, "ws1");
    52→    workspace = await createTestWorkspace(runtime.baseUrl, user);
    53→
    54→    // Given a task with status "ready"
    55→    const taskTitle = `Implement input validation ${Date.now()}`;
    56→    const task = await createReadyTask(
    57→      runtime.surreal,
    58→      workspace.workspaceId,
    59→      { title: taskTitle, status: "ready" },
    60→    );
    61→
    62→    // Surface 1: Task Popup -- "Assign to Agent" button visible
    63→    const detailBefore = await openTaskPopup(
    64→      runtime.baseUrl,
    65→      user,
    66→      workspace.workspaceId,
    67→      task.taskId,
    68→    );
    69→    expect(isAssignButtonVisible(detailBefore)).toBe(true);
    70→
    71→    // When I assign the task to an agent
    72→    const assignment = await assignTaskToAgent(
    73→      runtime.baseUrl,
    74→      user,
    75→      workspace.workspaceId,
    76→      task.taskId,
    77→    );
    78→    expect(assignment.agentSessionId).toBeDefined();
    79→
    80→    // Simulate agent transitioning from spawning to active
    81→    await simulateAgentActive(runtime, assignment.agentSessionId);
    82→
    83→    // Then the popup shows a status badge "Agent working"
    84→    const detailAfterAssign = await openTaskPopup(
    85→      runtime.baseUrl,
    86→      user,
    87→      workspace.workspaceId,
    88→      task.taskId,
    89→    );
    90→    expect(getAgentBadgeText(detailAfterAssign)).toBe("Agent working");
    91→
    92→    // Simulate agent completing work (transitions to idle)
    93→    await simulateAgentCompletion(runtime, assignment.agentSessionId);
    94→
    95→    // Surface 2: Governance Feed -- review-ready item appears
    96→    const feed = await getGovernanceFeed(
    97→      runtime.baseUrl,
    98→      user,
    99→      workspace.workspaceId,
   100→    );
   101→    const feedResult = findFeedItemForTask(feed, taskTitle);
   102→    expect(feedResult).toBeDefined();
   103→    expect(feedResult!.tier).toBe("review");
   104→    expect(
   105→      feedItemHasActions(feedResult!.item, ["Review", "Abort"]),
   106→    ).toBe(true);
   107→
   108→    // Surface 3: Agent Review View -- task title and accept
   109→    const review = await openReviewView(
   110→      runtime.baseUrl,
   111→      user,
   112→      workspace.workspaceId,
   113→      assignment.agentSessionId,
   114→    );
   115→    expect(reviewShowsTaskContent(review, taskTitle)).toBe(
   116→      true,
   117→    );
   118→
   119→    // When I accept the agent's work
   120→    const acceptResult = await acceptAgentWork(
   121→      runtime.baseUrl,
   122→      user,
   123→      workspace.workspaceId,
   124→      assignment.agentSessionId,
   125→    );
   126→    expect(acceptResult.accepted).toBe(true);
   127→
   128→    // Task status remains "ready" — accept completes the session, not the task
   129→    const finalStatus = await getTaskStatus(runtime.surreal, task.taskId);
   130→    expect(finalStatus).toBe("ready");
   131→  }, 60_000);
   132→
   133→  // US-2.3
   134→  it("rejects agent work with feedback and sees agent resume", async () => {
   135→    const runtime = getRuntime();
   136→    user = await createTestUser(runtime.baseUrl, "ws2");
   137→    workspace = await createTestWorkspace(runtime.baseUrl, user);
   138→
   139→    // Given a task assigned to an agent with completed work
   140→    const task = await createReadyTask(
   141→      runtime.surreal,
   142→      workspace.workspaceId,
   143→      { title: `Fix login bug ${Date.now()}`, status: "ready" },
   144→    );
   145→    const assignment = await assignTaskToAgent(
   146→      runtime.baseUrl,
   147→      user,
   148→      workspace.workspaceId,
   149→      task.taskId,
   150→    );
   151→    await simulateAgentCompletion(runtime, assignment.agentSessionId);
   152→
   153→    // When I reject the work with feedback
   154→    const rejectResult = await rejectWithFeedback(
   155→      runtime.baseUrl,
   156→      user,
   157→      workspace.workspaceId,
   158→      assignment.agentSessionId,
   159→      "Please add input validation for email field",
   160→    );
   161→    expect(rejectResult.rejected).toBe(true);
   162→    expect(rejectResult.continuing).toBe(true);
   163→
   164→    // Then the task popup badge shows "Agent working"
   165→    const detailAfterReject = await openTaskPopup(
   166→      runtime.baseUrl,
   167→      user,
   168→      workspace.workspaceId,
   169→      task.taskId,
   170→    );
   171→    expect(getAgentBadgeText(detailAfterReject)).toBe("Agent working");
   172→  }, 60_000);
   173→
   174→  // US-1.2
   175→  it("surfaces agent error as blocking feed item", async () => {
   176→    const runtime = getRuntime();
   177→    user = await createTestUser(runtime.baseUrl, "ws3");
   178→    workspace = await createTestWorkspace(runtime.baseUrl, user);
   179→
   180→    // Given a task assigned to an agent
   181→    const taskTitle = `Refactor auth module ${Date.now()}`;
   182→    const task = await createReadyTask(
   183→      runtime.surreal,
   184→      workspace.workspaceId,
   185→      { title: taskTitle, status: "ready" },
   186→    );
   187→    const assignment = await assignTaskToAgent(
   188→      runtime.baseUrl,
   189→      user,
   190→      workspace.workspaceId,
   191→      task.taskId,
   192→    );
   193→
   194→    // When the agent encounters an error
   195→    await simulateAgentError(
   196→      runtime,
   197→      assignment.agentSessionId,
   198→      "Out of memory during compilation",
   199→    );
   200→
   201→    // Then the governance feed shows a blocking item
   202→    const feed = await getGovernanceFeed(
   203→      runtime.baseUrl,
   204→      user,
   205→      workspace.workspaceId,
   206→    );
   207→    const feedResult = findFeedItemForTask(feed, taskTitle);
   208→    expect(feedResult).toBeDefined();
   209→    expect(feedResult!.tier).toBe("blocking");
   210→    expect(feedResult!.item.reason).toContain(
   211→      "Out of memory during compilation",
   212→    );
   213→  }, 60_000);
   214→});
   215→

1→/**
     2→ * Orchestrator UI Acceptance Test Kit
     3→ *
     4→ * Extends the orchestrator-test-kit with UI-surface-specific helpers.
     5→ * Tests invoke through the same HTTP driving ports as backend tests,

     7→ *
     8→ * Driving ports used:
     9→ *   - POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/assign (assign task to agent)
    10→ *   - GET  /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id (session status)
    11→ *   - GET  /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/review (review data)
    12→ *   - POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/accept (accept work)
    13→ *   - POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/reject (reject with feedback)
    14→ *   - POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:id/abort (abort session)
    15→ *   - GET  /api/workspaces/:ws/feed (governance feed)
    16→ *   - GET  /api/entities/:entityId (entity detail for task popup)
    17→ */
    18→
    19→import {
    20→  setupOrchestratorSuite,
    21→  createTestUser,

    37→  type TestTask,
    38→  type TestProject,
    39→  type AssignmentResponse,

    43→import type {
    44→  GovernanceFeedResponse,
    45→  GovernanceFeedItem,
    46→  EntityDetailResponse,
    47→} from "../../../app/src/shared/contracts";
    48→
    49→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50→// Re-exports for convenience
    51→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    52→
    53→export {
    54→  setupOrchestratorSuite,
    55→  createTestUser,
    56→  createTestWorkspace,
    57→  createReadyTask,
    58→  createTestProject,
    59→  assignTaskToAgent,
    60→  getSessionStatus,
    61→  acceptAgentWork,

    67→  fetchRaw,

    69→
    70→export type {
    71→  OrchestratorTestRuntime,

    87→ * This is the driving port for Surface 1.
    88→ */
    89→export async function openTaskPopup(
    90→  baseUrl: string,
    91→  user: TestUser,
    92→  workspaceId: string,
    93→  taskId: string,
    94→): Promise<EntityDetailResponse> {
    95→  const entityId = `task:${taskId}`;
    96→  return fetchJson<EntityDetailResponse>(
    97→    `${baseUrl}/api/entities/${encodeURIComponent(entityId)}?workspaceId=${encodeURIComponent(workspaceId)}`,
    98→    { headers: user.headers },
    99→  );
   100→}
   101→
   102→/**
   103→ * Checks whether the task popup would show an assign button.
   104→ * Business rule: assign button visible when task status is "ready" or "todo"
   105→ * and no active agent session exists.
   106→ */
   107→export function isAssignButtonVisible(
   108→  detail: EntityDetailResponse,
   109→): boolean {
   110→  const status = detail.entity.data.status as string | undefined;
   111→  const hasActiveSession = detail.agentSession !== undefined;
   112→  return (
   113→    detail.entity.kind === "task" &&
   114→    (status === "ready" || status === "todo") &&
   115→    !hasActiveSession
   116→  );
   117→}
   118→
   119→/**
   120→ * Checks whether the task popup would show a review button.
   121→ * Business rule: review button visible when agent session is idle.
   122→ */
   123→export function isReviewButtonVisible(
   124→  detail: EntityDetailResponse,

   126→  return detail.agentSession?.orchestratorStatus === "idle";

   129→/**
   130→ * Extracts the agent status badge text from entity detail.
   131→ */
   132→export function getAgentBadgeText(
   133→  detail: EntityDetailResponse,
   134→): string | undefined {
   135→  const session = detail.agentSession;
   136→  if (!session) return undefined;
   137→
   138→  const statusMap: Record<string, string> = {
   139→    spawning: "Starting agent...",
   140→    active: "Agent working",
   141→    idle: "Review ready",
   142→    completed: "Agent done",
   143→    error: "Agent error",
   144→    aborted: "Aborted",
   145→  };
   146→  return statusMap[session.orchestratorStatus];
   147→}

   150→ * Extracts file change count from entity detail agent session data.
   151→ */
   152→export function getFileChangeCount(
   153→  detail: EntityDetailResponse,
